zh.lifeSummary: Jyllian Jane Knapp於1472年出生。她於1499年與William Knapp在英國英格蘭素福爾可巴爾金結婚。他們至少是10個兒子和1個女兒的父母。她去世於1552年7月28日之前在英國英格蘭素福爾可布洛，埋葬在英國。

zh-hans.lifeSummary: Jyllian Jane Knapp于1472年出生。她于1499年与William Knapp在英国英格兰素福爾可巴爾金结婚。他们至少是10个儿子和1个女儿的父母。她去世于1552年7月28日之前在英国英格兰素福爾可布洛，埋葬在英国。

nameMeanings.name: Knapp
nameMeanings.attribution: Dictionary of American Family Names © Patrick Hanks 2003, 2006.
meanings: German: occupational or status name from the German word Knapp(e), a variant of Knabe ‘young unmarried man’. In the 15th century this spelling acquired the separate, specialized meanings ‘servant, apprentice’, and ‘miner’. This surname is also found elsewhere in central Europe, e.g. in Czechia and Slovakia, where it is more commonly spelled Knap (compare 3 below).
German: in Franconia, a nickname for a dexterous or skillful person, of the same ultimate origin as 1 above.
Germanized or Americanized form of Polish, Czech, Slovak, Rusyn, and Slovenian   Knap  , a surname of ultimately German origin (see 1 above).

variants.name: Knappe
meanings: Some characteristic forenames:  German Klaus, Rainer, Siegfried, Wolf.
German: variant of   Knapp  .

variants.name: Knap
meanings: Polish, Czech, Slovak, Rusyn, and Slovenian: of German origin (see   Knapp   1), meaning e.g. ‘pageboy, armor bearer’ (Czech), ‘weaver’ (Polish, Slovak, and Rusyn), ‘wool comber’ (Slovak and Slovenian), and ‘miner’ (Slovenian and Czech).
Jewish (eastern Ashkenazic): variant of   Knapp   6.

variants.name: Knabe
meanings: Some characteristic forenames:  German Erna, Gerhard, Helmut, Volker.

German: status name for a young man or a page, from Middle High German knabe. In aristocratic circles this term denoted a page or squire (a youth destined to become a knight), while among artisans it referred to a journeyman's assistant or (as a short form of Lehrknabe) ‘apprentice’. In the 15th century a semantic split between Knabe and its variants Knape, Knapp(e) resulted in Knabe meaning ‘boy’ and Knapp(e) ‘servant, apprentice’, and ‘miner’ in modern German.

variants.name: Canup
meanings: Americanized form of North German   Knapp   or   Knopp  .

variants.name: Knupp
meanings: German: variant of   Knapp   or   Knopp  .

variants.name: Knibbs
meanings: English: variant, with post-medieval excrescent -s, of Knibb, which may be related to Knapp or Nobbs or Knipe .

variants.name: Knapper
meanings: English: topographic name from Middle English knap(p)er(e), an agent derivative of knap(pe) ‘knob, hillock’, for someone who lived by a hilltop or hillock; compare Knapp .
German (also Knäpper): habitational name for someone from either of two places in Westphalia named Knapp.
German (Knäpper): unflattering nickname from an agent derivative of knappen ‘to be stingy’ or, in some places, ‘to grab or snatch’.

variants.name: Knapke
meanings: Some characteristic forenames:  German Aloys, Florian.
German: from a pet form of   Knapp  .

nameMeanings.name: Jyllian
nameMeanings.attribution: Dictionary of First Names © Patrick Hanks and Flavia Hodges 1990, 2003, 2006.
meanings: Variant spelling of   Gillian .

variants.name: Gillian
meanings: Variant of   Julian  , from which it was differentiated in spelling only in the 17th century.

nameMeanings.name: Jane
nameMeanings.attribution: Dictionary of First Names © Patrick Hanks and Flavia Hodges 1990, 2003, 2006.
meanings: Originally a feminine form of John , from the Old French form Je(h)anne. Since the 17th century it has proved the most popular of the feminine forms of John, ahead of Joan and Jean . It now also commonly occurs as the second element in combinations such as Sarah-Jane. In Britain it is still one of the most frequent of all girls' names. It is not a royal name, but was borne by the tragic Lady Jane Grey ( 1537–54 ), who was unwillingly proclaimed queen in 1553 , deposed nine days later, and executed the following year. Seventy years earlier, the name had come into prominence as that of Jane Shore , mistress of King Edward IV and subsequently of Thomas Grey , 1st Marquess of Dorset, Lady Jane's grandfather. Jane Shore's tribulations in 1483 at the hands of Richard III , Edward's brother and successor, became the subject of popular ballads and plays, which may well have increased the currency of the name in the 16th century. A 19th-century influence was its use as the name of the central character in Charlotte Brontë's novel Jane Eyre ( 1847 ). From 1932 to 1959 it was used as the name of a cheerful and scantily clad beauty whose adventures were chronicled in a strip cartoon in the Daily Mirror. It is also borne by the American film stars Jane Russell ( 1921–2011 ) and Jane Fonda ( b. 1937 ).

variants.name: Jayne
meanings: Elaborated spelling of   Jane  , a form popularized no doubt by the American actress  Jayne Mansfield  (  Vera Jayne Palmer ,  1933–67 ) and British ice-dancer  Jayne Torvill  (  b. 1957 ).

variants.name: Jaine
meanings: Variant spelling of   Jane .

variants.name: Jain
meanings: Originally a feminine form of John , from the Old French form Je(h)anne. Since the 17th century it has proved the most popular of the feminine forms of John, ahead of Joan and Jean . It now also commonly occurs as the second element in combinations such as Sarah-Jane. In Britain it is still one of the most frequent of all girls' names. It is not a royal name, but was borne by the tragic Lady Jane Grey ( 1537–54 ), who was unwillingly proclaimed queen in 1553 , deposed nine days later, and executed the following year. Seventy years earlier, the name had come into prominence as that of Jane Shore , mistress of King Edward IV and subsequently of Thomas Grey , 1st Marquess of Dorset, Lady Jane's grandfather. Jane Shore's tribulations in 1483 at the hands of Richard III , Edward's brother and successor, became the subject of popular ballads and plays, which may well have increased the currency of the name in the 16th century. A 19th-century influence was its use as the name of the central character in Charlotte Brontë's novel Jane Eyre ( 1847 ). From 1932 to 1959 it was used as the name of a cheerful and scantily clad beauty whose adventures were chronicled in a strip cartoon in the Daily Mirror. It is also borne by the American film stars Jane Russell ( 1921–2011 ) and Jane Fonda ( b. 1937 ).

historicEvents.title: Book of Common Prayer
historicEvents.description: Book of Common Prayer, a product of the English Reformation, was published in 1549 for assistance in the administration of the sacraments and other rites and ceremonies of the church according to the use of the Church of England. The book outlined morning, evening, and communion prayers and orders for baptism and marriage, thus making England a truly Protestant state.

en.lifeSummary: Jyllian Jane Knapp was born in 1472. She married William Knapp in 1499, in Barking, Suffolk, England, United Kingdom. They were the parents of at least 10 sons and 1 daughter. She died before 28 July 1552, in Brome, Suffolk, England, United Kingdom, and was buried in England.

fr.lifeSummary: Jyllian Jane Knapp est née en 1472. Elle a épousé William Knapp en 1499, à Barking, Suffolk, Angleterre, Royaume-Uni. Ils ont eu au moins 10 fils et 1 fille. Elle est décédée avant 28 juillet 1552, à Brome, Suffolk, Angleterre, Royaume-Uni, et a été enterrée à Angleterre.

de.lifeSummary: Jyllian Jane Knapp wurde 1472 geboren. Sie hat 1499 in Barking, Suffolk, England, United Kingdom William Knapp geheiratet. Sie hatten mindestens 10 Söhne und 1 Tochter. Sie starb vor 28. Juli 1552 in Brome, Suffolk, England, United Kingdom und wurde in England begraben.

it.lifeSummary: Jyllian Jane Knapp è nata il nel 1472. Lei sposato William Knapp nel 1499, nel Barking, Suffolk, Inghilterra, Regno Unito. Erano genitori di almeno 10 figli e 1 figlia. Morì prima del 28 luglio 1552, nel Brome, Suffolk, Inghilterra, Regno Unito, e fu sepolta a England.

ja.lifeSummary: Jyllian Jane Knappは1472年に生まれた。1499年にイギリスイギリススフォルクバーキングでWilliam Knappと結婚した。彼らは少なくとも10人の息子と1人の娘の親であった。彼女は1552年7月28日以前にイギリスイギリススフォルクブロメで死去し，イギリスにて埋葬された。

ko.lifeSummary: Jyllian Jane Knapp(은)는 1472년 태어났다. 그녀는 1499년 영국 잉글랜드 수폴크 바킨그에서 William Knapp(와)과 결혼했다. 그들은 적어도 열 명의 아들과 한 명의 딸을 두었다. 그녀는 1552년 7월 28일 이전 영국 잉글랜드 수폴크 브롬에서 사망했으며 영국에 묻혔다.

pt.lifeSummary: Jyllian Jane Knapp nasceu em 1472. Ela casou-se com William Knapp em 1499, em Barking, Suffolk, Inglaterra, United Kingdom. Eles tiveram pelo menos 10 filhos e 1 filha. Ela faleceu antes 28 de julho de 1552, em Brome, Suffolk, Inglaterra, United Kingdom, e foi sepultada em Inglaterra.

ru.lifeSummary: Jyllian Jane Knapp родилась в 1472 г. Она вышла замуж за человека по имени William Knapp в 1499 г. в местности баркинг, суффолк, Англия, Великобритания. У них было по крайней мере 10 сыновей и 1 дочь. Она умерла перед 28 июля 1552 г. в местности бром, суффолк, Англия, Великобритания и была похоронена в местности Англия.

es.lifeSummary: Jyllian Jane Knapp nació en 1472. Se casó con William Knapp en 1499, en Barking, Suffolk, Inglaterra, Reino Unido. Fueron padres de por lo menos 10 hijos y 1 hija. Murió antes de 28 de julio de 1552, en Brome, Suffolk, Inglaterra, Reino Unido, y fue sepultada en Inglaterra.
